seo.title: Building Workflow Automation with LangChain + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build workflow automation with LangChain + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building workflow automation with LangChain enables complex multi-step agents that integrate APIs, handle conditional logic, and incorporate human approvals for reliable task execution. However, these systems are prone to hallucinations, infinite tool loops, context overflows, and formatting inconsistencies that can lead to failed workflows or security risks. Vex provides runtime verification to ensure action correctness, permissions, idempotency, and rollback capabilities, making your automations production-ready.

architectureOverview.heading: Workflow Automation Architecture with LangChain
architectureOverview.description:
A typical LangChain workflow automation architecture centers around an AgentExecutor that orchestrates tools, chains, and conditional routing. The agent receives tasks via a router chain, calls external APIs through custom tools, applies business logic via LLM-driven decision nodes, and pauses for human-in-the-loop approvals using LangGraph's checkpointing. RAG retrieval provides context for decision-making, while output parsers ensure structured responses feed into subsequent steps.

The flow typically starts with task ingestion, followed by parallel tool execution for data gathering, sequential processing for validation, and conditional branching based on LLM reasoning. Human approval gates use persistent state management to resume workflows. Observability is added via LangSmith tracing, but runtime verification requires wrapping critical invoke() calls.

This architecture scales to enterprise automation but introduces risks around tool misuse, state corruption, and unhandled edge cases that Vex mitigates through structured verification.

risks.name: Hallucination in RAG retrieval chains
risks.description: Agent hallucinates incorrect API endpoints or parameters during tool selection
risks.mitigation: Verify retrieved context relevance and tool parameter correctness before execution

risks.name: Tool call loops in agent executors
risks.description: Agent enters infinite loop calling the same API repeatedly
risks.mitigation: Track execution state and enforce max iterations with idempotency checks

risks.name: Context window overflow in long conversations
risks.description: Accumulated conversation history exceeds token limits, causing truncation
risks.mitigation: Monitor token usage and summarize/archive conversation state

risks.name: Inconsistent output formatting across chain steps
risks.description: Downstream steps fail due to malformed JSON from previous chain outputs
risks.mitigation: Enforce Pydantic/JSON schema validation at each chain boundary

implementation.title: Multi-Step Agent with Custom Tools
implementation.description: Create an agent executor with API tools and conditional routing for workflow steps
implementation.code:
from langchain.agents import AgentExecutor, create_react_agent
from langchain.tools import tool
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from langchain.prompts import PromptTemplate

@tool
def update_user_status(user_id: str, status: str) -> str:
    """Update user workflow status via API"""
    return f"Updated user {user_id} to {status}

llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o-mini")
tools = [update_user_status]

prompt = PromptTemplate.from_template("Answer using tools: {input}")
agent = create_react_agent(llm, tools, prompt)
agent_executor = AgentExecutor(agent=agent, tools=tools, verbose=True)

result = agent_executor.invoke({"input": "Update user 123 to approved"})

implementation.title: Human-in-the-Loop with LangGraph
implementation.description: Implement approval checkpointing using LangGraph state persistence
implementation.code:
from langgraph.graph import StateGraph, END
from typing import TypedDict, Annotated
import operator

class WorkflowState(TypedDict):
    user_id: str
    status: str
    approved: bool

    messages: Annotated[list, operator.add]

def approval_node(state: WorkflowState) -> WorkflowState:
    return {"messages": ["Pending human approval for user " + state["user_id"]]}

def human_decision_node(state: WorkflowState) -> str:
    if state.get("approved", False):
        return "approved"
    return "pending"

workflow = StateGraph(WorkflowState)
workflow.add_node("approval", approval_node)
workflow.add_node("decision", human_decision_node)
workflow.set_entry_point("approval")
app = workflow.compile(checkpointer=MemorySaver())

implementation.title: TypeScript Agent with Tool Binding
implementation.description: Define workflow agent with structured tool calls in TypeScript

implementation.title: Conditional Routing Chain
implementation.description: Route workflow steps based on LLM decision making
implementation.code:
from langchain.chains.router import MultiPromptChain
from langchain.chains.llm import LLMChain
from langchain.prompts import PromptTemplate

llm = ChatOpenAI()

routing_prompt = PromptTemplate.from_template("Classify task: {input}")
routing_chain = LLMChain(llm=llm, prompt=routing_prompt)

destination_chains = {
    "api_call": LLMChain(...),
    "human_review": LLMChain(...)
}

router_chain = MultiPromptChain(
    router_chain=routing_chain,
    destination_chains=destination_chains,
    default_chain=LLMChain(...)
)

result = router_chain.invoke({"input": "Process payment approval"})
